steps.title: Recognize the complementary angle relationship
steps.content: The problem states that angles $R$ and $S$ sum to $90°$, making them complementary angles. A key trigonometric property: when two angles are complementary, the sine of one angle equals the cosine of the other.

steps.title: Apply the relationship to find cos(S)
steps.content:
Since $R + S = 90°$, we apply the complementary angle property: $\sin(R) = \cos(S)$. We are given $\sin(R) = \frac{\sqrt{15}}{4}$, therefore:
$$\cos(S) = \frac{\sqrt{15}}{4}$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
